posts.author: sheekhuj
posts.text: I’m looking to get some institutional and industry perspectives on the scalability and structural viability of the pivot LIC Housing Finance (LICHFL) announced in their recent earnings calls. The Backdrop & Valuation Anomaly: Following its asset quality struggles during FY22–23, LICHFL’s loan book sequential growth gone down to ~5% YoY . Because of this the stock trades at a multiple of ~5.3x P/E —a massive discount compared to other NBFCs. The Management Catalyst: To break free from this competitive “prime salaried” trap where universal banks hold a massive cost-of-funds edge, management has…

posts.author: sambandham82
posts.text: The asset quality worsened between 2012-2018 due to massive boom in infra, power, steel,etc., in 2004-2007 mainly. The corporate balance sheet also had huge debt during that period. But now corporate balance sheet is very healthy. So if we are considering overall banking, it requires a credit upmove to happen before asset quality starts detoriating. I think its a long way from here.
